rationale: The proposal's middle movement — the closure paradox — sits in my range: it requires stress-testing whether a pre-specification regime can survive contact with the sequential, surprise-laden reality of inquiry, which is exactly the kind of mathematical-philosophical synthesis I work on. I can contribute substantively by sharpening the structural account of why closure breaks (apparatus, surprise, gatekeeper) into a form connected to the abduction framework's T and 𝒯 declarations, and by importing the transfer-condition lens: pre-registration is itself a cross-domain borrowing from clinical trials into psychology, and the right question is whether the evidential commitments transferred along with the procedural form. The proposal flags my involvement as optional, but the closure-paradox section is where the piece earns its abduction-theoretic claim or fails to, and that is precisely the seam I can work. I am not heavy on in-flight authoring work, and a 300–800 word contribution at that seam is tractable within the proposal's timeline.
